ddls:
CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the language */,
  "language_family" TEXT /* Language family the language belongs to */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 639-3 code for the language */,
  "native_speakers" INTEGER /* Number of native speakers */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographic region where the language is spoken */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the language is spoken */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "parameter_type" TEXT /* Type of parameter (e.g., phonological, grammatical) */,
  "parameter_category" TEXT /* Category of parameter (e.g., morphological, syntactic) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "language_families" (
  "family_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language family */,
  "family_name" TEXT /* Name of the language family */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the language family */,
  "parent_family_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent language family (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("family_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the source */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of publication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the database */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the database was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "accessed_table" TEXT /* Table that was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "language_family_membership" (
  "language_id" TEXT /* ID of the language */,
  "family_id" INTEGER /* ID of the language family */,
  "membership_type" TEXT /* Type of membership (e.g., primary, secondary) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id", "family_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "language_id" TEXT /* ID of the language */,
  "parameter_id" TEXT /* ID of the parameter */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the parameter for the language */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("language_id") REFERENCES languages ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "language_id" TEXT /* ID of the language */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment text */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the comment was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("language_id") REFERENCES languages ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
column_names: Language family the language belongs to
Geographic region where the language is spoken
Country where the language is spoken
Unique identifier for each language family
ID of the parent language family (if applicable)
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin)
Unique identifier for each access event
ID of the user accessing the database
Date when the database was accessed
Value of the parameter for the language
Timestamp when the version was created
Timestamp when the comment was created
